        - command:
          - mongod
          - --config
          - /etc/mongod/mongod.conf
          - --replSet=mongodb-replica
          - --keyFile=/keyfile/keyfile

          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
          name: mongod

        {"apiVersion":"v1","data":{"mongod.conf":"storage:\n  dbPath: /data/db\n  wiredTiger:\n    engineConfig:\n      journalCompressor: zlib\nnet:\n  bindIpAll: true\nreplication:\n  replSetName: mongodb-replica\nsecurity:\n  authorization: enabled\n  keyFile: /etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile\nsystemLog:\n  verbosity: 1\noperationProfiling:\n  mode: slowOp\n  slowOpThresholdMs: 200\n"},"kind":"ConfigMap","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"mongod-config","namespace":"mongodb"}}
    creationTimestamp: "2026-07-04T00:57:29Z"
    name: mongod-config
    namespace: mongodb
    resourceVersion: "2139619"
    uid: 1dd00c57-8c8f-485d-9053-4adfbadec564
- apiVersion: v1
  data:
    init-replica.sh: |
      #!/bin/bash
      set -e

      # Wait until mongod is accepting connections (unauthenticated, before auth is enforced)
      until mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval "db.adminCommand('ping')" --quiet 2>/dev/null; do
        echo "Waiting for mongod..."
        sleep 2
      done

      # Only the first pod initiates the replica set
      if [[ "$HOSTNAME" == "mongodb-replica-0" ]]; then
        echo "Initiating replica set..."
        mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval '
          rs.initiate({
            _id: "mongodb-replica",
            members: [
              { _id: 0, host: "mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017" },
              { _id: 1, host: "mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017" },
              { _id: 2, host: "mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017" }
            ]
          })
        ' --quiet 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait for PRIMARY
        echo "Waiting for PRIMARY..."
        until mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval "rs.status().myState" --quiet 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^1$"; do
          sleep 3
        done

        echo "Creating admin user..."
        mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval '
          db = db.getSiblingDB("admin");
          db.createUser({
            user: "admin-user",
            pwd: "admin123password",
            roles: [{ role: "root", db: "admin" }]
          });
        ' --quiet 2>/dev/null || true

        echo "Creating app user..."
        mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin --eval '
          db = db.getSiblingDB("appdb");
          db.createUser({
            user: "app-user",
            pwd: "app123password",
            roles: [{ role: "readWrite", db: "appdb" }]
          });
        ' --quiet 2>/dev/null || true

        echo "Init complete."
      fi

      # Keep container running
      tail -f /dev/null
  kind: ConfigMap
  metadata:
    annotations:
      kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
        {"apiVersion":"v1","data":{"init-replica.sh":"#!/bin/bash\nset -e\n\n# Wait until mongod is accepting connections (unauthenticated, before auth is enforced)\nuntil mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval \"db.adminCommand('ping')\" --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null; do\n  echo \"Waiting for mongod...\"\n  sleep 2\ndone\n\n# Only the first pod initiates the replica set\nif [[ \"$HOSTNAME\" == \"mongodb-replica-0\" ]]; then\n  echo \"Initiating replica set...\"\n  mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval '\n    rs.initiate({\n      _id: \"mongodb-replica\",\n      members: [\n        { _id: 0, host: \"mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017\" },\n        { _id: 1, host: \"mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017\" },\n        { _id: 2, host: \"mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017\" }\n      ]\n    })\n  ' --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n\n  # Wait for PRIMARY\n  echo \"Waiting for PRIMARY...\"\n  until mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval \"rs.status().myState\" --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null | grep -q \"^1$\"; do\n    sleep 3\n  done\n\n  echo \"Creating admin user...\"\n  mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval '\n    db = db.getSiblingDB(\"admin\");\n    db.createUser({\n      user: \"admin-user\",\n      pwd: \"admin123password\",\n      roles: [{ role: \"root\", db: \"admin\" }]\n    });\n  ' --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n\n  echo \"Creating app user...\"\n  mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin --eval '\n    db = db.getSiblingDB(\"appdb\");\n    db.createUser({\n      user: \"app-user\",\n      pwd: \"app123password\",\n      roles: [{ role: \"readWrite\", db: \"appdb\" }]\n    });\n  ' --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n\n  echo \"Init complete.\"\nfi\n\n# Keep container running\ntail -f /dev/null\n"},"kind":"ConfigMap","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"mongodb-init-scripts","namespace":"mongodb"}}
    creationTimestamp: "2026-07-04T00:52:25Z"
    name: mongodb-init-scripts
    namespace: mongodb
    resourceVersion: "2138157"
    uid: 4519fee0-c7a2-45e4-9fca-1027ccafaba2

        {"apiVersion":"v1","data":{"mongod.conf":"storage:\n  dbPath: /data/db\n  wiredTiger:\n    engineConfig:\n      journalCompressor: zlib\nnet:\n  bindIpAll: true\nreplication:\n  replSetName: mongodb-replica\nsecurity:\n  authorization: enabled\n  keyFile: /etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile\nsystemLog:\n  verbosity: 1\noperationProfiling:\n  mode: slowOp\n  slowOpThresholdMs: 200\n"},"kind":"ConfigMap","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"mongod-config","namespace":"mongodb"}}
    creationTimestamp: "2026-07-04T00:57:29Z"
    name: mongod-config
    namespace: mongodb
    resourceVersion: "2139619"
    uid: 1dd00c57-8c8f-485d-9053-4adfbadec564
- apiVersion: v1
  data:
    init-replica.sh: |
      #!/bin/bash
      set -e

      # Wait until mongod is accepting connections (unauthenticated, before auth is enforced)
      until mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval "db.adminCommand('ping')" --quiet 2>/dev/null; do
        echo "Waiting for mongod..."
        sleep 2
      done

      # Only the first pod initiates the replica set
      if [[ "$HOSTNAME" == "mongodb-replica-0" ]]; then
        echo "Initiating replica set..."
        mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval '
          rs.initiate({
            _id: "mongodb-replica",
            members: [
              { _id: 0, host: "mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017" },
              { _id: 1, host: "mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017" },
              { _id: 2, host: "mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017" }
            ]
          })
        ' --quiet 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait for PRIMARY
        echo "Waiting for PRIMARY..."
        until mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval "rs.status().myState" --quiet 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^1$"; do
          sleep 3
        done

        echo "Creating admin user..."
        mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval '
          db = db.getSiblingDB("admin");
          db.createUser({
            user: "admin-user",
            pwd: "admin123password",
            roles: [{ role: "root", db: "admin" }]
          });
        ' --quiet 2>/dev/null || true

        echo "Creating app user..."
        mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin --eval '
          db = db.getSiblingDB("appdb");
          db.createUser({
            user: "app-user",
            pwd: "app123password",
            roles: [{ role: "readWrite", db: "appdb" }]
          });
        ' --quiet 2>/dev/null || true

        echo "Init complete."
      fi

      # Keep container running
      tail -f /dev/null
  kind: ConfigMap
  metadata:
    annotations:
      kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
        {"apiVersion":"v1","data":{"init-replica.sh":"#!/bin/bash\nset -e\n\n# Wait until mongod is accepting connections (unauthenticated, before auth is enforced)\nuntil mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval \"db.adminCommand('ping')\" --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null; do\n  echo \"Waiting for mongod...\"\n  sleep 2\ndone\n\n# Only the first pod initiates the replica set\nif [[ \"$HOSTNAME\" == \"mongodb-replica-0\" ]]; then\n  echo \"Initiating replica set...\"\n  mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval '\n    rs.initiate({\n      _id: \"mongodb-replica\",\n      members: [\n        { _id: 0, host: \"mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017\" },\n        { _id: 1, host: \"mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017\" },\n        { _id: 2, host: \"mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017\" }\n      ]\n    })\n  ' --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n\n  # Wait for PRIMARY\n  echo \"Waiting for PRIMARY...\"\n  until mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval \"rs.status().myState\" --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null | grep -q \"^1$\"; do\n    sleep 3\n  done\n\n  echo \"Creating admin user...\"\n  mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval '\n    db = db.getSiblingDB(\"admin\");\n    db.createUser({\n      user: \"admin-user\",\n      pwd: \"admin123password\",\n      roles: [{ role: \"root\", db: \"admin\" }]\n    });\n  ' --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n\n  echo \"Creating app user...\"\n  mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin --eval '\n    db = db.getSiblingDB(\"appdb\");\n    db.createUser({\n      user: \"app-user\",\n      pwd: \"app123password\",\n      roles: [{ role: \"readWrite\", db: \"appdb\" }]\n    });\n  ' --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null || true\n\n  echo \"Init complete.\"\nfi\n\n# Keep container running\ntail -f /dev/null\n"},"kind":"ConfigMap","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"mongodb-init-scripts","namespace":"mongodb"}}

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture:

**Current state:**
- The ConfigMap `mongod-config` has:
  - verbosity: 1
  - slowOpThresholdMs: 200
  - journalCompressor: zlib

- But the RUNNING pods (all 3) are using:
  - verbosity: 2
  - slowOpThresholdMs: 400
  - journalCompressor: **snappy**

Wait, but the mounted config file shows verbosity: 1, slowOpThresholdMs: 200, journalCompressor: zlib. Yet the pods report verbosity: 2, slowOpThresholdMs: 400, journalCompressor: snappy.
This is a contradiction. The getCmdLineOpts shows what was used at startup, while the mounted file reflects the current ConfigMap content. The ConfigMap must have been updated after the pods started, so they're still running with the older configuration values from when they initially launched. So the ConfigMap was created at 2026-07-04T00:57:29Z with zlib compression, but the StatefulSet restart timestamp is earlier—2026-07-03T20:03:54-05:00, which converts to 2026-07-04T02:03:54Z in UTC. Let me recalculate the timezone conversion since that's not matching up correctly. The pods themselves are only a couple minutes old based on their age, so I need to figure out the actual sequence of events here. slowOpThresholdMs: 200
```

This is the current mounted configuration from the ConfigMap, but the running mongod process is using different settings entirely. The pods were started with verbosity:2, slowOpMs:400, and snappy compression, then the ConfigMap was updated to verbosity:1, slowOpMs:200, and zlib compression. The mounted file reflects the new ConfigMap values, but mongod is still running with its original startup configuration since it hasn't been restarted to pick up the changes.

The task is asking me to adjust the configuration by increasing verbosity by one level (1→2), doubling the slow operation threshold (200→400), and switching journal compression to zlib. So the target configuration should be verbosity:2, slowOpThresholdMs:400, and journalCompressor:zlib—which happens to match what the running pods were originally started with. I need to update the ConfigMap to these values and then perform a rolling restart of the StatefulSet since journal compression changes require a restart to take effect. So I need to update the ConfigMap with verbosity set to 2 and slowOpThresholdMs set to 400, while the journalCompressor is already correctly configured as zlib. After a rolling restart of the pods, the running instances will pick up these new settings from the ConfigMap.

For the rolling restart, I'm relying on StatefulSet's default behavior which updates pods from highest to lowest ordinal. Since this is a 3-member replica set, the secondaries restart first while the primary remains available, and when the primary eventually restarts, the replica set automatically elects a new primary from the remaining members, ensuring continuous data availability throughout the process.

I should also verify whether the journal compressor change from snappy to zlib requires a restart or can be applied at runtime. In MongoDB's WiredTiger engine, the journal compression algorithm is baked into the write-ahead log files, so changing it necessitates a mongod restart. WiredTiger can handle starting with a different compressor and will read existing journal files appropriately, which should be fine for this fresh setup with minimal data.

So my plan is straightforward: update the ConfigMap with the new verbosity and slowOpMs values, trigger a rolling restart via StatefulSet annotation, wait for all three pods to come back up, then verify the settings across all members. I need to clarify that the ConfigMap represents the baseline state for this task scenario, not what's currently running on the pods.

content.thinking:
ConfigMap is updated correctly. Now I need to do a rolling restart of the StatefulSet pods.

For a 3-member replica set, the safest approach is to restart secondaries first, then the primary. Since we're using a StatefulSet with `OrderedReady` policy, rolling update goes from the highest ordinal down (2, 1, 0). 

If mongodb-replica-0 is the PRIMARY, it will be last to restart, which is good. When it restarts, there will be a brief election and the remaining nodes (already restarted) will elect a new primary.

Let me trigger a rolling restart using `kubectl rollout restart`.

content.text: ConfigMap is correct. Now trigger a rolling restart so all pods pick up the new config (verbosity=2, slowOpMs=400, zlib).
